en.extract:
Zoe's imperial pigeon or Zoe imperial pigeon (Ducula zoeae), also known as the banded imperial pigeon, is a species of bird in the family Columbidae.
It is found in New Guinea.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest, subtropical or tropical mangrove forest, and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.
It is named after Zoë Lesson (fl. 1810), the wife of French ornithologist René-Primevère Lesson, who described it.

'''Zoe's imperial pigeon''' or '''Zoe imperial pigeon''' ('''''Ducula zoeae'''''), also known as the '''banded imperial pigeon''', is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Columbidae]].
It is found in [[New Guinea]].
Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]], subtropical or tropical [[mangrove forest]], and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]].

It is named after Zoë Lesson ([[floruit|fl.]] 1810), the wife of French ornithologist [[René-Primevère Lesson]], who described it.<ref>{{cite book|last1=Jobling|first1=James A.|title=Helm Dictionary of Scientific Bird Names|url=https://archive.org/details/Helm_Dictionary_of_Scientific_Bird_Names_by_James_A._Jobling|date=2010|publisher=Christopher Helm|location=London|page=[https://archive.org/details/Helm_Dictionary_of_Scientific_Bird_Names_by_James_A._Jobling/page/n414 414], s.v. zoeae}}</ref>
